macro_segments.title: Introduction and Framework: Three Rabbinic Discussions on Yayin Nesech and Benefit

micro_segments.title: Opening remarks and transition to Daf 64
Introduction to three gatherings of rabbis discussing interactions between Yisrael, Ger Toshav, and Oved Kochavim
Gemara structure: repeated yativ (sitting) formulations and the role of Rav Nachman

macro_segments.title: First Case: Hiring a Jew to Break Barrels of Yayin Nesech

micro_segments.title: Scenario and problem: non-Jew hires Jew to break barrels of yayin nesech; conflict of interest regarding preservation
Two possible approaches: wages forbidden due to interest in preservation, or permitted because reducing tifla
Rav Nachman's ruling: he may break and be blessed; bringing support from Beraita on kilayim
Detailed analysis of kilayim case and Rabbi Akiva's exegetical method on Vayikra 19:19
Resolving the Kilayim case: only applies when not receiving payment; introduces Rav Yehuda's principle on free gifts
Principle derived: reducing issur is permitted (lechalot issur muttar); application to both Rav Yehuda and Rabbi Akiva

macro_segments.title: Second Case: Money from Avodat Kochavim in the Hands of a Non-Jew

micro_segments.title: Second seating of rabbis: question about status of wages from avodat kochavim held by oved kochavim
Rav Nachman's ruling: dmei avodat kochavim in the hands of oved kochavim is permitted
Case of non-Jews converting: selling avodat kochavim and using proceeds; ambiguity whether this proves the ruling
Alternative proof: creditor scenario with conditional payment (אם אמר לו המתן לי)
Analysis: difference between immediate payment and delayed payment; interest in preservation of avodat kochavim
Rav Sheshet's explanation: in conditional case, Jew desires preservation of avodat kochavim

macro_segments.title: Third Case: Division of Inheritance Between Ger and Oved Kochavim

micro_segments.title: Scenario: convert and idol-worshiper brothers inheriting from father; can they divide idolatrous goods?
Ger's proposed division: split property by type (idolatry to brother, money to self, etc.)
Problem: once goods come into ger's possession, division is forbidden
Rav Bar Ula's limitation: ruling applies only to avodat kochavim that can be divided by shards (e.g., gold)
Question about yayin nesech: how does it apply to division by shards? Reference to cheres Adriani
Concern about ger's interest in preservation; Rav Papa's leniency for gerim based on fear of return to idolatry
Baraita: ger and oved kochavim can only divide by inheritance, not by partnership

macro_segments.title: Fourth Case: Can a Ger Toshav Annul the Status of Avodat Kochavim?

micro_segments.title: Question: can a ger toshav annul avodat kochavim status?
Rav Nachman's principle: one who worships can annul (dephalach mevatel delo phalach lo mevatel)
Challenge from Baraita: Jew finds avodat kochavim in marketplace; can tell non-Jew to cancel before taking possession
Clarification of Baraita: non-Jew can cancel his own or friend's avodat kochavim (עובדה ושאינו עובדה)
Debate: does עובדה (worshiper) vs. שאינו עובדה include ger toshav or only refer to non-Jews?
Conclusion: both terms refer to oved kochavim; examples with Pe'or and Markulis

macro_segments.title: Definition and Status of Ger Toshav

micro_segments.title: Opening definition: Baraita presents multiple opinions on who is a ger toshav
Rabbi Meir's view: ger toshav accepted upon himself before three chaverim not to serve avodat kochavim
Chachamim's view: ger toshav accepts seven mitzvot of bnei Noach; distinction from regular ben Noach
Alternative third view: ger toshav accepts all Torah mitzvot except prohibition on nevelot
Status of ger toshav's wine and oil: limited time deposit permitted, long-term deposit forbidden; wine and oil treated equally
Machlokes: Rabban Shimon holds wine of ger toshav is yayin nesech; others permit drinking it
Clarification: for all other matters ger toshav is like oved kochavim; limited to reshut (domain) law for eruvin
Explanation of reshut: eruv creation requires Jewish ownership/annulment; non-Jews and ger toshav cannot participate
Conclusion: ger toshav cannot annul avodat kochavim; limited only to reshut/eruv context

topical_tags.term: dephalach mevatel delo phalach lo mevatel (worship principle)
avodat kochavim (idol worship service/wages from it)
Rav Yehuda (on free gifts to non-Jews)
cheres Adriani (clay vessel used for wine concentration)
lechalot issur muttar (reducing forbidden thing is permitted)
sheva mitzvot bnei Noach (seven commandments of Noah's descendants)
